question_html:
Correct the dangling modifier.

(a) Running to the bus stop, the bag fell off my shoulder.
‎(b) While looking for my keys, the phone rang.
‎(c) While walking in the rain, the umbrella broke.
‎(d) To complete the project on time, extra effort was needed.
‎(e) After sleeping for ten hours, the headache was still there.
solution_html:
(a) Running to the bus stop, I dropped my bag.

Or, As I ran to the bus stop, my bag fell off my shoulder.
‎(b) While looking for my keys, I heard the phone ring.

Or, As I looked for my keys, the phone began to ring.
‎(c) While walking in the rain, I broke my umbrella.

Or, As I walked in the rain, my umbrella broke.
‎(d) To complete the project on time, we needed extra effort.

Or, We had to put in extra effort to complete the project on time.
‎(e) After sleeping for ten hours, I still had a headache.

Or, Even after sleeping for ten hours, I still had a headache.

Marks Distribution:

(0.5× 5) = 2.5 marks for making correction of dangling modifier.

question_html:
Make meaningful sentences with the following words:

Arduous, Frugal, Candid, Elated, Revoke
solution_html:
Arduous: Climbing the hill was an arduous task.

Frugal: He lived a frugal life to save money.

Candid: She gave a candid answer in court.

Elated: The team was elated after the victory.

Revoke: The authority will revoke your license for breaking rules.

Marks Distribution:

(0.5×5)=2.5 marks for making any meaningful correct sentences.

question_html: Write a paragraph on ‘Causes and Effects of Consumerism.’
solution_html:
Causes and Effects of Consumerism

Consumerism is the social and economic focus on the acquisition of goods and services. It occurs when people prioritize material possessions over needs, often influenced by advertising and social trends. Excessive consumerism can lead to environmental degradation, resource depletion, and financial stress, while fostering wasteful lifestyles. It also affects mental well-being by creating unrealistic desires and social pressure. Governments, educational institutions, and media can promote responsible consumption by raising awareness, encouraging sustainable practices, and regulating misleading advertisements. Balancing consumption with sustainability ensures economic growth without harming society or the environment.

Marks Distribution:

0.5 mark for writing Title “Causes and Effects of Consumerism”.

01 mark for writing meaning of consumerism.

02 marks for writing effects of consumerism on society, environment, and individuals.

1.5 marks for writing efforts to reduce harmful consumerism.

[Instructions:

* Students must write it within one single para. If it becomes more than one para, 50% marks have to be deducted from the obtained marks.

* 0.5 mark will be deducted for one or two grammar or spelling mistakes, 01 mark for three or more grammar or spelling mistakes.]
